description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Hiroshima Prefecture (広島県, Hiroshima-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [çi.ɾo.ɕi.ma, -maꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Chūgoku region of Honshu.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

description: town in Iwami district, Tottori prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Iwami (岩美町, Iwami-chō) is a town located in Iwami District, Tottori Prefecture, Japan.

description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Shimane Prefecture (島根県, Shimane-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [ɕiꜜ.ma.ne, ɕi.ma.neꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Chūgoku region of Honshu.

description: conflict between the Russian and Japanese empires from 1904 to 1905
description_wiki: The Russo-Japanese War (8 February 1904 – 5 September 1905) was fought between the Russian Empire and the Empire of Japan over rival imperial ambitions in Manchuria and the Korean Empire.

description: capital city of the province of British Columbia, Canada
description_wiki: Victoria is the capital city of the Canadian province of British Columbia, located on the southern tip of Vancouver Island off Canada's Pacific coast.

description: global war originating in Europe, 1914–1918
description_wiki: World War I, or the First World War (28 July 1914 – 11 November 1918), also known as The Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies (or Entente) and the Central Powers.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: California () is a state in the Western United States that lies on the Pacific Coast.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Wyoming (  wy-OH-ming) is a landlocked state in the Mountain West subregion of the Western United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Nevada (  nə-VAD-ə; Spanish: [neˈβaða] ) is a landlocked state in the Western United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: New Mexico is a landlocked state in the Southwestern region of the United States.

description: city in Ehime Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Iyo (Japanese: 伊予市, Hepburn: Iyo-shi) is a city located in Ehime Prefecture, Japan.

description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Ehime Prefecture (愛媛県, Ehime-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [eꜜ.çi.me, e.çi.meꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located on the island of Shikoku.

description: fourth largest island of Japan
description_wiki: Shikoku (四国, Shikoku; pronounced [ɕi̥ꜜ.ko.kɯ, ɕi̥.koꜜ.kɯ] , lit. 'four provinces') is the smallest of the four main islands of Japan.

description: period of Japanese history from 1912 to 1926, under the reign of Emperor Taishō

entity: United States Secretary of the Treasury
description: Head of the United States Department of the Treasury
description_wiki: The United States secretary of the treasury is the head of the United States Department of the Treasury, and is the chief financial officer of the federal government of the United States.

description: designated city in Osaka Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Osaka (Japanese: 大阪市, Hepburn: Ōsaka-shi; pronounced [oː.sa.kaꜜ.ɕi]; commonly just 大阪, Ōsaka [oː.sa.ka] ) is a designated city in the Kansai region of Honshu in Japan.

description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Nara Prefecture (奈良県, Nara-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [naꜜ.ɾa, na.ɾaꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Kansai region of Honshu.

description: city in and county seat of Yakima County, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Yakima ( or ) is a city in and the county seat of Yakima County, Washington, United States, and the state's 11th most populous city.

description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Kagoshima Prefecture (鹿児島県, Kagoshima-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [ka.ɡo.ɕi.ma, -maꜜ.keɴ, ka.ŋo-]) is a prefecture of Japan located on the island of Kyushu and the Ryukyu Islands.

description: samurai
description_wiki: Kusunoki Masashige (Japanese: くすのき まさしげ、, Kyūjitai: 楠木 正成; Japanese pronunciation: [kɯ̥.sɯꜜ.no.kʲi], 1294 – 4 July 1336), or   Dai Nankô (大楠公; [naꜜŋ.koː],  lit. 'Lord Kusunoki'), was a Japanese military commander and samurai of the Kamakura period remembered as the ideal loyal samurai.

description: Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Minatogawa Shrine (湊川神社, Minatogawa-jinja) is a Shinto shrine in Chūō-ku, Kobe, Japan, roughly on the site of the Battle of Minatogawa established in 1872.

description: 1894–1895 war between the Qing dynasty and the Empire of Japan over influence in Joseon, fought chiefly in Joseon
description_wiki: The First Sino-Japanese War (25 July 1894 – 17 April 1895), or the First China–Japan War, was a conflict between the Qing dynasty of China and the Empire of Japan primarily for influence over Korea.

description: Japanese era from October 1868 to July 1912
head of state of Japan
description_wiki: The emperor of Japan is the hereditary monarch and head of state of Japan.

description: visiting the same shrine or temple a hundred times to pray to the gods or Buddha

surface_forms: atomic bomb was dropped over the city
after the atomic bomb was dropped
description: bombardment of Hiroshima in Japan carried out by the United States using an atomic bomb at the end of the Second World War on 6 August 1945
1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.
